snp3.8:2.2:
by which those who are born will not die.
Beings are subject to death
snp3.8:2.3: even when they attain old age.
snp3.8:3.2: whose downfall, whose danger is falling,
snp3.8:4.1: As clay vessels made by a potter
snp3.8:4.4: so too life heads to death.
snp3.8:5.3: All come under the sway of death;
snp3.8:5.4: all have death as their end.
snp3.8:7.1:
See:
Even while relatives are looking on,
snp3.8:7.3: mortals are one by one led away
snp3.8:8.1: In this way is the world afflicted
snp3.8:8.3: and so the enlightened don’t grieve,
snp3.8:8.4: knowing the way of the world.
snp3.8:10.3: —confused, harming yourself— any use could be gained
snp3.8:10.4: the prudent would do it as well.
snp3.8:11.2: do you gain peace of awareness.
snp3.8:12.3: Not in that way are the dead protected.
snp3.8:13.3: Bewailing one whose time is done,
snp3.8:13.4: you fall under the sway of grief.
snp3.8:14.2: people arriving in line with their actions:
snp3.8:14.3: falling under the sway of death,
snp3.8:15.2: it always turns out other than that.
snp3.8:15.4: See the way of the world.
snp3.8:16.1: Even if a person lives a century
snp3.8:16.3: he’s parted from his community of relatives,
snp3.8:16.4: he abandons his life right here.
snp3.8:17.3: seeing the dead one whose time is done,
snp3.8:18.1: Just as one would put out a burning refuge
snp3.8:18.6:
like the wind, a bit of cotton fluff.
Seeking your own happiness, you should pull out your own arrow:
your own
